meaning: vt.给...权利(或资格),给...题名,给...称号v.授权,授权
examples: This ticket does not entitle you to travel first class.
If the warranty is limited, the terms may entitle you to a replacement or refund.
Golden parachutes entitle them to a full year's salary if they get booted out of the company.
But he said that shouldn't entitle the government to force him to buy a car.
That, in turn, can sometimes entitle them to, say, a free coffee or pizza.
